tests_added.name: test_merge_49_words
tests_added.edge_case: 49 words (one below TINY_DIVISION_WORDS=50) MUST merge with sibling

tests_added.name: test_merge_50_words_standalone
tests_added.edge_case: Exactly 50 words must NOT merge (threshold is strictly < 50, inclusive boundary)

tests_added.name: test_merge_51_words_standalone
tests_added.edge_case: 51 words (one above threshold) must NOT merge

tests_added.name: test_merge_1_word
tests_added.edge_case: Single-word chunk (trivially tiny) merges with sibling

tests_added.name: test_merge_0_words_with_sibling
tests_added.edge_case: 0 Arabic words (non-Arabic content) still triggers merge (0 < 50)

tests_added.name: test_split_4999_words_no_split
tests_added.edge_case: 4999 words (one below OVERSIZED_DIVISION_WORDS=5000) must NOT split

tests_added.name: test_split_5000_words_no_split
tests_added.edge_case: Exactly 5000 words must NOT split (threshold is strictly > 5000)

tests_added.name: test_split_5001_words_must_split
tests_added.edge_case: 5001 words (one above threshold) MUST split into ≥2 chunks each ≤5000 words

tests_added.name: test_split_10001_words_recursive_three_chunks
tests_added.edge_case: 10001 words requires recursive splitting into ≥3 chunks (V-P1-4)

tests_added.name: test_la1_at_79_point_9_percent
tests_added.edge_case: 79.9% coverage (799/1000 chars) must NOT trigger LA-1 — falls to LA-2

tests_added.name: test_la_two_layers_each_50_percent
tests_added.edge_case: 50%/50% two-layer split → LA-2 (outermost layer wins: SHARH > MATN)

tests_added.name: test_la_three_layers_each_33_percent
tests_added.edge_case: 33%/33%/34% three-layer split → LA-3 (ambiguous, flag for consensus)

tests_added.name: test_layer_gap_5_chars_repaired
tests_added.edge_case: 5-char gap (= 5, exact boundary) → auto-repair + EX-A-003 WARNING

tests_added.name: test_layer_gap_6_chars_fatal
tests_added.edge_case: 6-char gap (> 5) → NOT repaired → I-AC-2 FATAL (one above repair limit)

learnings: All three threshold predicates are strict: merge uses <50 (not <=50), split uses >5000 (not >=5000), gap repair uses <=5 (not <5). The exactly-at-boundary values behave as the non-triggering case for merge/split, and as the triggering case for gap repair.
Existing tests covered gap=2 (repair) and gap=10 (fatal) but missed the exact boundary gap=5 (repair) and gap=6 (fatal). This session added those.
Existing LA tests covered exactly 80% and 70% but missed 79.9% and 80.1%. The 79.9% test provides a tighter probe that would catch off-by-one errors in >= vs > comparisons.
The 0-word merge case (non-Arabic content) confirms that the merge predicate treats non-Arabic content as tiny (0 < 50) — it does not special-case zero-word chunks.
The 5000-word no-split test is the most valuable new test: it guards against inadvertent changes of the strict > to >=, which would cause valid 5000-word chunks to be split unnecessarily.
